I turn mere men into heroes.

WHAT DO YOU TEACH

First we lay the foundation, I teach them how to set goals through deep journaling. You can't get where you want to go if you don't know for starters where you want to go. Through deep journaling you also teach yourself to think in an orderly, structured and logical manner and can develop actionable steps to achieve your goals, basically reverse engineer such goals into daily habits and tasks. That's where the timetable comes in. I teach them how to build their timetables to get consistent at the habits that will take them from where they are to where they want to go. Usually we all want a strong aesthetic body and be healthy, have energy and vitality, so I teach them how to get consistent at the gym and the secrets of nutrition (must people are clueless about nutrition, for example most people think vegetables are healthy but vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like oxalates and phytic acid, oxalates bind to iron, zing and magnesium in your blood and render them useless for you body to assimilate, that's why they are called anti-nutrients).
⟨BUBBLE⟩
We all want wisdom and knowledge to make sound decisions and to know which path to take, so I teach them to get consistent and reading which is the most profound way to acquire knowledge there is.
⟨BUBBLE⟩
We all want to have good mental health, control over out attention and a personal understanding of the structure of reality, that's why I teach them how to meditate and eleveate their vibrational frequency. How to do gratitude journaling consistently and to expand their consciousness.
⟨BUBBLE⟩
And last but no least we need a purpose, a way, a path, a mission. I teach them how to find it and follow it. Build their online businesses, help other people (potentially as educational content creators) and once they are ready and if I see they are worthy I take them into my inner circle and get them ready for the next stage. I'm putting together a team of high quality, powerful men into self-improvement making online more than $10K per month to kickstart a civilizational project, at the beginning a nomadic tribe of men into self-improvement off the grid, soverign. But to get into that place they need first to overcome The Trial. The initiation ritual. A 3 months journey by sea from England to Karelia, northen Russia, where we'll hunt wolves and each men will have to undergo a series of tests to prove he can become part of our brotherhood.

So basically, first i can say that i am 15 years old. Maybe a little shocking for someone. I have always felt more mature than everyone else around me. Now many people ask, why so early? My whole life i have hated the idea of school and the system itself. Sure, if you want to be a doctor, you have to study. But in my case i really want to achieve freedom and live by this quote; Do something you love, and you will never work again.

So i got on self improvement maybe 1 year ago, but it has been lots of ups and downs. 1 month you are so dedicated and then the other you completely forget that you even are on this thing.

Alright enough of that ill tell you more about me now.

My name is Danielis, i live in Norway and i want to create one of the biggest AI companies in the world.

7-8 months ago my life took a big turn. I was just beginning to do basketball coaching, i landed my first client at only 14 years old. No big deal but 47$ a month. And then i got struck with a prolapse in my spine. I couldnt walk, play basketball, jump, run, go out with friends. NOTHING!! It was the worst month of my life (october 2024).

Lucky for me, i found my girlfriend at a halloween party and she pretty much saved me. Whilst i had nothing to do, she would come to me and spend time with me. The basketball coaching basically died that month. I still talk to my client, but no payments taken from him yet.

This is what I do for young men:

# Stage 1:

First we lay the foundation, I teach them how to set goals through deep journaling. You can't get where you want to go if you don't know for starters where you want to go. Through deep journaling you also teach yourself to think in an orderly, structured and logical manner and can develop actionable steps to achieve your goals, basically reverse engineer such goals into daily habits and tasks. That's where the timetable comes in. I teach them how to build their timetables to get consistent at the habits that will take them from where they are to where they want to go.

# Stage 2:

Usually we all want a strong aesthetic body and be healthy, have energy and vitality, so I teach them how to get consistent at the gym and the secrets of nutrition (must people are clueless about nutrition, for example most people think vegetables are healthy but vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like oxalates and phytic acid, oxalates bind to iron, zing and magnesium in your blood and render them useless for you body to assimilate, that's why they are called anti-nutrients).

We all want wisdom and knowledge to make sound decisions and to know which path to take, so I teach them to get consistent and reading which is the most profound way to acquire knowledge there is.

We all want to have good mental health, control over out attention and a personal understanding of the structure of reality, that's why I teach them how to meditate and eleveate their vibrational frequency. How to do gratitude journaling consistently and to expand their consciousness.

# Stage 3:

And last but no least we need a purpose, a way, a path, a mission. I teach them how to find it and follow it. Build their online businesses, help other people (potentially as educational content creators) and once they are ready and if I see they are worthy I take them into my inner circle and get them ready for the next stage.

# Stage 4:

I'm putting together a team of high quality, powerful men into self-improvement making online more than $10K per month to kickstart a civilizational project, at the beginning a nomadic tribe of men into self-improvement off the grid, soverign. But to get into that place they need first to overcome The Trial. The initiation ritual. A 3 months journey by sea from England to Karelia, northen Russia, where we'll hunt wolves and each men will have to undergo a series of tests to prove he can become part of our brotherhood.

Deliberate practice:
1.Define: what is the problem (what do the pins on the lock look like)
2.Hypothesis: Come up with a hypothesis to solve it.
3.Act: Try it straight away to either succeed at solving it or fail as quickly as possible and acquire critical information.

Reflecion Upon Your Practice:
4.Analysis: Reflect upon if the thesis that you acted out was correct and accurate at solving the problem. Did it solved it? Fantastic! It didn’t? Can you identify what’s wrong and reiterate on the next time you act?
5.Hypothesis reconstruction: Come up with a different hypothesis based on the little information collected from your practice.
6.Second reiteration: Try again and keep repeating this loop until you obtain the desired results.
7.Data collection: From the insights that you’ve got and the critical information that you have gathered start building a body of knowledge that shall guide you in case you forget or that can guide and help others to obtain the same results.

Theoretical Knowledge applied to your practice:
8.Seek: actively searching for the required information in the places it can be found
9.Receive: your sensory system coming into contact with the information
10.Process: your brain processing the information
11.Judge: judging whether the information is true or not (is it objectively accurate?)
12.Decide and Act upon it (test out the 3rd hypothesis born out of somebody else’s insights and/or body’s of knowledge): conscious decision to act on the information, deciding what to do about it and implement.

beautiful man !

I'm near Barcelona in Pierra in Amma's ashram

life in an ashram is different depending on the Guru and the Ashram itself

the One I'm currently in has very strict discipline and heavy load of volunteering, we work in an olive farm daily regardless of the hot temperatures

the one I'm currently in pushed you to the edge of your performance in term od body and mind

But I've been I some that are more like 5 star hotels with jacuzzi and tennis courts

whatever the ashram one thing is clear you feel a sense of peace and home

what prevented you from living in one so far?

I guess it wasn't meant to be. At that moment I coudn't get my visa because of an issue with the institution that was supposed to provide the reason for the visa in the first place, it was a students visa and that institution didn't have the credentials of the autonomus community of Andalucia to do that, and by the time I was going to try to get another place my host family coudn't afford more money to pay for the other university, so we gave up on that after more than 9 months trying. I would have carried on but I can't force others to put themselves into a bad situation because of me. So I figure it was fine and I would just carry on with my life until my next opportunity.
⟨BUBBLE⟩
After that I was planning to go to China and on the first opportunity I had I'd go to mount Wutai and join a taoist temple there. But my travel to China wasn't possible either because the plain tickets got too expensive and my budget back then shrunk, so I cound't afford it.
⟨BUBBLE⟩
I ended up traveling to Malaysia and kickstarting my journeys as a digital nomad, making money online and experiencing life in the South Asian East 6 months after my 3 year long relationship ended with a girl I truly thought I'd marry and start a live with her. But it was truly for the best. The amount of growth I've been through since then has been truly amazing...
⟨BUBBLE⟩
After Malaysia I traveled to Canggu, Bali in Indonesia, which is where I am right now. I am planning at some point to go to India and I might get to experience life in an ashram there, who knows?

I have perhaps too many recommendations when it comes to good transformative books. But depends on what you’re looking for right now… what area do you feel or know you should be growing yourself in? Need discipline, structure, a profound understanding of behavioral science and how to implement good habits that are conductive to high levels of performance? Start with Atomic Habits, then the 7 Habits of Highly Effective People to complement what is missing on the first one and Deep Work by Cal Newport to learn how to enter into a flow state and do the highest leverage tasks with the outmost efficiency in the shortest spans of time possible.

If you still need more on productivity and habits there’s a lot more but I don’t want to overflow you with way too many books because you’ll end up reading none of them and confused at the end. Better to have 3 or 4 good books and study them relentlessly to deeply understand them and apply the teachings contained in them.

But don’t wait until you have the information to apply. Raw action solves it all. While you learn theoretically still apply with whatever limited knowledge you might have at the moment and keep track of what you’re doing and the outcomes.

There are three components to the META-Skill:

1-Deliberate practice.
2-Reflection upon your practice (deep journaling is a very powerful tool here).
3-Theoretical knowledge applied to your practice.

To explain all this in more depth and detail and be able to deploy a proper action step plan or roadmap to follow to structure a powerful self-improvement/work routine we would have to jump on a call of 1 to 3 hours and I’d have to share with you a series of resources I used to share with my self-improvement coaching clients that… now that I’m thinking you could borrow some of that and use it with your clients as well adapting it to yourself if you see it fit. If that sounds like something you’d like to do that’s the way I see I could provide you with way much more value.
I charge for those kind of sessions €333 and even though I’m not really doing it anymore I believe you’d benefit greatly from it if you are serious about applying all this and moving radically the needle forward, brother.
Let me know how it goes and regardless of anything I hope this helps.

My story:
PRE 2023 -
Average young man, unhappy in life, had dreams of making money online, got nowhere, was motivated by Tate and etc, but could not take action, it was cause of bad mental health.
2023 -
Hit the lowest point of my life on Halloween 2023, decided to change my life as my eyes got messed up by a flashlight, I got eye strain could not distract myself any more I watched a full mental health guide from a YouTuber Hamza it changed my life, I tried to start business this year as well earlier, but failed cause of poor mental health.
2024 -
Started a business called "Copy Compose." Grinded hard for months,didn't realise that I made no success cause I got no joy or happiness from my work I made some decent money online that year but still was unhappy.
2025 -
I am now making money online with my passion and purpose of mental health, and I truly feel happy, fulfilled even beyond money, my mission is to help other entrepreneurs who have money but still ain't happy or fulfilled or want more money, and the biggest reason there like that is unhealed trauma.

Bro, that's quite a story. I resonate the shit out of it! After 4 years of a chronic depression, from 17 to 21, when I was thinking about killing myself I discovered self-improvement and the whole concept of mental health with Jordan Peterson and Hamza and it truly changed my life forever.

This is what I do for young men:

# Stage 1:

First we lay the foundation, I teach them how to set goals through deep journaling. You can't get where you want to go if you don't know for starters where you want to go. Through deep journaling you also teach yourself to think in an orderly, structured and logical manner and can develop actionable steps to achieve your goals, basically reverse engineer such goals into daily habits and tasks. That's where the timetable comes in. I teach them how to build their timetables to get consistent at the habits that will take them from where they are to where they want to go.

# Stage 2:

Usually we all want a strong aesthetic body and be healthy, have energy and vitality, so I teach them how to get consistent at the gym and the secrets of nutrition (must people are clueless about nutrition, for example most people think vegetables are healthy but vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like oxalates and phytic acid, oxalates bind to iron, zing and magnesium in your blood and render them useless for you body to assimilate, that's why they are called anti-nutrients).

We all want wisdom and knowledge to make sound decisions and to know which path to take, so I teach them to get consistent and reading which is the most profound way to acquire knowledge there is.

We all want to have good mental health, control over out attention and a personal understanding of the structure of reality, that's why I teach them how to meditate and eleveate their vibrational frequency. How to do gratitude journaling consistently and to expand their consciousness.

# Stage 3:

And last but no least we need a purpose, a way, a path, a mission. I teach them how to find it and follow it. Build their online businesses, help other people (potentially as educational content creators) and once they are ready and if I see they are worthy I take them into my inner circle and get them ready for the next stage.

# Stage 4:

I'm putting together a team of high quality, powerful men into self-improvement making online more than $10K per month to kickstart a civilizational project, at the beginning a nomadic tribe of men into self-improvement off the grid, soverign. But to get into that place they need first to overcome The Trial. The initiation ritual. I can help you with the business side of things, mainly on how to build the funnel, how to structure the offer. This is the best framework usually: You start runnign your MVP as a coaching service for a sort of low ticket, you gain momentum and confidence, build up some testimonials and acquire enough knowlege of your niche and your dream avatar so you can actually develop a course. Usually the sweet spot is after 15 clients with results but it can be more or less depending on some factors. Then you'll realize that your niche (if you're focusing on one niche and one niche only instead of trying to help everyone that comes your way, I'll explain in more detail why it doesn't work and why you can't hit 10K per month doing that) has certain recurrent problems, your brain will start doing some patterm recognition, I can teach you the structure to build the course, you set it up on skool (it's the most effective and easier option) but the kowledge of the content will develop organically in your brain. Then you set up the coures and with enough traffic from you YT channel you sell it and now the coaching becomes a high ticket thing becasue you have developed such authority on your niche. Then you run adds for both the course and your high ticket, one at a time and after you have gained some knowledge on how to do that or either you buy a tool to do it or hire a marketing agency, there's many options here. At this point you scale from 10 or 30K per month to 100K which is usually the ceiling for low-ticket courses combined with high ticket coaching, top 150K or so. After that there's other levels, but let's concern ourselves with the current one. How much are you making currently (how many clients and how much are you charging per client?) and what's the traffic that you get usually from one video?

I got first into self-improvement to escape pain. I went through a 4 year long chronic depression that led me to suicidal thoughts and the way out of it I found it through self-improvement, building a timetable for myself and focusing on developing body (through working out), mind (by reading) and spirit (through meditation and gratitude journaling) and after only 2 weeks I was out of the depression and I decided to never stop improving consistently my life, 1% better every single day.
⟨BUBBLE⟩
Takeaways from my clients and my work? Well there’s a lot to tell about that. I don’t want to extend myself endlessly for the sake of time and efficiency so I’ll try to say only one takeaway and summarize it as much as I can here: Basically I realized that the biggest constraint to deliver to my clients their desired outcome was… *drumrolls… themselves! I knew I could take them from where they were to where they wanted to be in (let’s say) 3 months, but still would take me 7 to actually get to a meaningful outcome with them. The main problem was that instead of them turning off their brains and following my instructions to the letter and doing the work they would procrastinate, fight back with me and force me to convince them instead of being efficient and just doing what I would teach them. The solution I found was instead of having monthly retainers with them was to create this pain behind them, this powerful motivator for them to act, which was a conditional guaranteed on a high ticket offer. Example: I’ll take you from point A to point B in X (timeframe) or I’ll give you a full refund (under the condition that you follow my instructions and do the work consistently [I got systems in place now for a more efficient accountability process]. And that would increase the success rate and reduce the amount of time it’d take me to deliver them results.
⟨BUBBLE⟩

I have transitioned now into business consulting for self-improvement coaches.
